file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.thinking:
The job is currently running and seems to be computing Pi successfully. The logs show tasks being processed. The job configuration already has:
- `serviceAccountName: spark-pi` (correct)
- `image: apache/spark:3.5.3` (correct, no "-nonexistent" suffix)
- `memory: "512Mi"` (correct)

However, the RBAC role is missing "pods" from the resources. The current job seems to be running successfully, but let me wait a bit to see if it completes.

Actually, let me re-read the task description more carefully. The task says:
I'm noticing the YAML file appears to have already been corrected—the image, service account, and memory values are all valid now, even though the original issues were the wrong image name with "-nonexistent", an incorrect service account reference, malformed memory format, and missing RBAC permissions for pods. The job is currently executing, so I should wait to see if it finishes successfully. then apply the RBAC fix to add the missing "pods" permission. The job is currently running, so I'll let it continue while I make this change, and then monitor whether it completes successfully or if it needs the additional permission.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].type}' 2>&1)
  ACTIVE=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.active}' 2>&1)
  SUCCEEDED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>&1)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>&1)
  echo "$(date '+%H:%M:%S') - status=$STATUS active=$ACTIVE succeeded=$SUCCEEDED failed=$FAILED"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Complete" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 15
done
input.description: Poll job status until complete or failed (up to 6 minutes)

description: Poll job status until complete or failed (up to 6 minutes)
